choices.description: Default V3 product app with Scene, SkiaViewer, Elmish, KeyboardInput, Layout, Controls, product tests, and product governance.
Headless Scene-only V3 product for scene/widget authoring.
Governed V3 product with Scene plus Testing helpers.
Sample-oriented V3 product with Scene, SkiaViewer, Elmish, and sample-pack content.

designSystem.description: Governing color policy for the generated product. 'wcag' (default) is byte-identical to today; 'ant' imprints the Ant design language and Ant contrast policy. Unknown values are rejected.

choices.description: WCAG 2.x contrast governance (default; no diff vs today's template).
Ant Design contrast expectations over the Ant-derived tokens.

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle scaffolding to emit alongside the generated product. 'spec-kit' (default) is byte-identical to today: it emits the full Spec Kit lifecycle workspace (.specify/, the project constitution, the .agents/ + .claude/ agent skill/context files, and the generated AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md agent-context tree). 'sdd' and 'none' suppress that entire gated lifecycle set while leaving the generated product (source, projects, product tests, docs, samples, and the designSystem overlay) untouched. Unknown values are rejected.

choices.description: Emit the full Spec Kit lifecycle workspace: .specify/, the project constitution, the .agents/ and .claude/ skill/context files, and the generated AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md agent-context tree (default; no diff vs today's template).
Suppress the gated lifecycle set; emit only the generated product. Intent: an external SDD lifecycle owner re-supplies the lifecycle scaffolding.
Suppress the gated lifecycle set; emit only the generated product. Intent: no lifecycle scaffolding is wanted. Same template-level output as 'sdd'.

rootNamespace.description: Compatibility parameter accepted by the template; generated project names are derived from --name.

productName.description: Product name conforming to the SDD scaffold-provider convention. When supplied, names the generated product (overrides --name/-n). Empty/whitespace is treated as not supplied and falls back to --name/-n.

packagePrefix.description: Package prefix reserved for future generated product packages.

initGit.description: Initialize a Git repository with an initial '[Spec Kit] Initial commit' and mark generated shell scripts executable after generation. Default false: generation is side-effect-free (no process, no repository). Unnecessary under the SDD scaffold path, which performs these steps itself.

feedback.description: Capture per-phase Spec Kit feedback into specs/<feature>/feedback/ (adds the after_* feedback hooks and the fs-gg-feedback-capture skill). Default false induces no diff.

sources.comment: Feature 204 (FR-004): the base product is UNGATED, but its bundled lifecycle agent-context content — `.agents/`, `.claude/`, and the agent-context `CLAUDE.md` (which the generated tree overwrites under spec-kit) — is part of the gated lifecycle set, so it is excluded here and re-emitted only under `lifecycle == "spec-kit"` by the two gated `template/base/.agents/`+`.claude/` sources below (preserving today's emit order: base agent-context first, then the repo-root `.agents/skills/` overwrites). README.md stays ungated but is made lifecycle-neutral (the spec-kit copy is re-supplied by the generated tree). Feature 060 (FR-003): the emitted docs/api-surface/**/*.fsi tree is the authoritative framework contract surface; copy it verbatim so the sourceName ('Product') substitution does not rewrite framework signatures (e.g. ProductDefect) in the generated reference. Feature 062 (FR-003/005/006): the generated docs/evidence-formats.md and docs/skillist-reference.md references and the authored docs/scaffold-map.md are copied verbatim (no sourceName substitution) so their governance tokens are not rewritten. Feature 108 (FR-020): docs/interactive-readiness.md is copied verbatim for the same reason (its skill names / readiness tokens must not be rewritten).

Feature 204: gated re-emit of the base agent skill/context tree (excluded from the ungated base source above). Placed before the repo-root `.agents/skills/` source so root still overwrites the base `fs-gg-project` skill exactly as today (byte-identical under spec-kit).

Feature 204: gated re-emit of the base .claude/ settings, hooks, and fs-gg-project skill (excluded from the ungated base source above). Placed before the repo-root `.agents/skills/`->.claude/skills/ source so root still overwrites the base `fs-gg-project` skill exactly as today.

Feature 128 (F3): the ant-only design-system overlay (the feedback no-diff precedent). Fires ONLY for designSystem == "ant"; no entry fires for wcag, so the default scaffold stays byte-identical to today (FR-003/SC-001). Copies a self-describing policy record + the Ant imprint as data; template/base/ is untouched.

postActions.description: Opt-in (--initGit true): make generated shell scripts executable and create the initial Git commit (skipped if already inside a repository).

manualInstructions.text: Run 'find . -type f \( -name "*.sh" -o -name "fake.sh" \) -exec chmod +x {} +' and then run 'git init && git add . && git commit -m "[Spec Kit] Initial commit"' if this project is not already inside a Git repository.

args.args: -c "find . -type f \( -name '*.sh' -o -name 'fake.sh' \) -exec chmod +x {} +; if ! command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo '[fs-gg-ui] git not found; skipped repository initialization' >&2; exit 0; fi; if ! git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree >/dev/null 2>&1; then git init -q && git add . && git -c user.name='FS GG UI Template' -c user.email='template@example.invalid' commit --allow-empty -q -m '[Spec Kit] Initial commit'; fi"

postActions.description: Opt-in (--initGit true): create the initial Git commit (skipped if already inside a repository).

manualInstructions.text: Run 'git init && git add . && git commit -m "[Spec Kit] Initial commit"' from the generated project root if this project is not already inside a Git repository.

args.args: -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "if (-not (Get-Command git -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { Write-Warning '[fs-gg-ui] git not found; skipped repository initialization'; exit 0 }; git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree 2>$null | Out-Null; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { git init -q; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit $LASTEXITCODE }; git add .; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit $LASTEXITCODE }; git -c user.name='FS GG UI Template' -c user.email='template@example.invalid' commit --allow-empty -q -m '[Spec Kit] Initial commit'; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit $LASTEXITCODE } }"
